lesson.name: Applied Nursing Practice — Skills, Pharmacology, Mental Health - Lesson 3 — Nursing Advanced Skills

meta.courseTitle: Applied Nursing Practice — Skills, Pharmacology, Mental Health

tasks.chapterTitle: Chapter 5: Insert Nasogastric and Feeding Tubes
Chapter 6: Manage Chest Tube Drainage Systems

messages.content:
This section provides an overview of advanced nursing procedures related to venous and arterial access, emphasizing evidence-based safety and clinical judgment [S.17].

### **Key Concepts in Vascular Access & Sampling**

*   **Blood Collection Methods:**
    *   **Venipuncture:** The primary method for collecting blood and inserting IV catheters; typically performed in the antecubital fossa [S.24, S.25].
    *   **Central Venous Access Devices (CVADs):** Used for long-term access and blood sampling from large veins in the chest, neck, or groin [S.26].
    *   **Capillary Blood Sampling:** Used for small-volume testing, such as bedside glucose monitoring, via finger or heel sticks [S.26].
    *   **Arterial Blood Sampling:** Performed by specialized personnel to assess acid-base balance (e.g., arterial blood gases). Due to higher risks of bleeding and ischemia, it is more invasive than venipuncture [S.27, S.28].
    *   **Arterial Lines:** Used for hemodynamically unstable patients requiring continuous arterial pressure monitoring and frequent blood draws. **Critical Safety Note:** Never infuse medications or fluids through an arterial line [S.28].

*   **Intravenous (IV) Therapy:**
    *   **Purpose:** The direct administration of fluids, electrolytes, medications, blood products, or nutrition into the bloodstream [S.29].
    *   **Clinical Utility:** Preferred when rapid onset of action is required or when the gastrointestinal tract is non-functional [S.29].
    *   **Best Practices:** Nurses must be diligent in infection control, site selection, and the use of appropriate catheter sizes to minimize complications and maintain patent access [S.23].

### **Clinical Standards & Implementation**
*   **Standards:** Content is guided by the Infusion Nurses Society standards, the NCLEX-RN Test Plan, and QSEN safety competencies [S.16, S.17].
*   **Clinical Judgment:** Nurses are expected to move beyond rote skills by recognizing cues, analyzing data, and evaluating outcomes to provide safe, patient-centered care [S.17].
*   **Documentation:** All procedures, including IV initiation and CVAD management, require strict documentation to ensure continuity and safety [S.5, S.6].

Lesson 3 — Nursing Advanced Skills - Presentation9 slides
Nursing Advanced Skills: Course Overview
  • Covers critical clinical procedures for registered nurses.
  • Aligned with NCLEX-RN standards and evidence-based practice.
  • Includes IV therapy, central lines, and ECG interpretation.

This textbook, *Nursing Advanced Skills*, is an open educational resource (OER) designed for prelicensure nursing students [S.9]. It serves as a comprehensive guide aligned with the Wisconsin Technical College System curriculum and the NCLEX-RN Test Plan [S.9].

### Key Details and Scope
*   **Purpose:** The text provides instruction on advanced clinical skills to build upon foundational knowledge established in the *Nursing Skills* OER [S.9].
*   **Content Areas:** The curriculum is divided into seven major parts:
    *   Initiating IV Therapy [S.5]
    *   Administering IV Push Medications [S.5]
    *   Administering Blood Products [S.6]
    *   Managing Central Lines [S.6]
    *   Inserting Nasogastric and Feeding Tubes [S.6, S.7]
    *   Managing Chest Tube Drainage Systems [S.7]
    *   Interpreting Basic ECG [S.7]

### Resources and Accessibility
*   **Development:** The project was supported by a $2.5 million grant from the U.S. Department of Education and developed by a team of nursing educators and professionals at Chippewa Valley Technical College [S.10, S.11].
*   **Licensing:** The content is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC-BY) license, allowing for free sharing and adaptation [S.12].
*   **Accessibility:** Digital versions are available for free, while affordable print versions are offered through collaborations with XanEdu and various bookstores [S.9].

### Summary of Open RN Nursing Advanced Skills: Standards and Conceptual Approach

This textbook utilizes a conceptual framework based on established nursing standards, research, and educational competencies to guide nursing practice.

**External Standards and Competencies**
The curriculum is aligned with several national bodies to ensure comprehensive and safe patient care:
* American Nurses Association (ANA): Sets the standards for practice and the Nursing Code of Ethics (p. 7).
* National Council of State Boards of Nursing (NCSBN): Incorporates NCLEX-RN test plans, updated every three years (p. 7).
* National League of Nursing (NLN): Provides competencies for nursing graduates to ensure quality care (p. 7).
* American Association of Colleges of Nursing (AACN): Defines core competencies for professional nursing education (p. 8).
* Infusion Nurses Society (INS): Establishes standards for IV therapy, blood administration, and central line management (p. 8).
* Quality and Safety Education for Nurses (QSEN): Focuses on essential knowledge, skills, and attitudes regarding safety and quality (p. 8).
* Advocacy and Regulation: The text references the Wisconsin Administrative Code for professional practice and Healthy People 2030 for health and well-being objectives (p. 9).

**Conceptual Approach**
The nursing curriculum prioritizes several key pedagogies:
* Evidence-Based Practice (EBP): Content is continuously updated with EBP and current clinical standards (p. 9).
* Clinical Judgment: Uses the NCSBN Clinical Judgment Measurement Model to teach students how to identify cues, prioritize hypotheses, and evaluate outcomes (p. 9).
* Inclusive Care: Promotes cultural humility and utilizes clear, inclusive language, including the use of singular "they" to avoid gender assumptions (p. 10).
* Innovation in Education: Employs open-source images, fair use, and open pedagogy, allowing students to contribute to the content (p. 10).
* Resources: Provides students with free supplementary videos, interactive activities, and both digital and affordable print versions of the text (p. 11).

**Clinical Application: IV Therapy and Venipuncture**
The text emphasizes that IV therapy is an invasive procedure requiring strict safety protocols (p. 15). Key technical aspects include:
* Methods of Blood Collection: Includes capillary sampling, peripheral venipuncture, and blood draws from Central Venous Access Devices (CVADs) (p. 16, 18).
* Arterial Access: Specialized procedures like arterial blood gas sampling and arterial line management are performed only by trained personnel. These lines are dedicated strictly to pressure monitoring and sampling; they must never be used for medication or fluid infusion (p. 20).
* Intravenous Therapy: Used for fluids, electrolytes, and medications. Proper management requires understanding fluid balance, with solutions categorized into isotonic, hypotonic, and hypertonic types (p. 21-22). Isotonic solutions, such as 0.9% normal saline, are used to restore vascular volume (p. 22).

### Summary of Part II: Administer IV Push Medications (pp. 91-130)

Part II covers the essential knowledge and clinical procedures required for the safe administration of intravenous (IV) push medications. The core components of this section include:

**1. Foundational Concepts (pp. 92-104)**
This section establishes the clinical framework for IV push therapy. It emphasizes the importance of understanding pharmacodynamics, drug compatibility, and the potential for rapid systemic effects when delivering medication directly into the bloodstream.

**2. Equipment Requirements (pp. 105-110)**
Nursing students are introduced to the necessary tools for safe medication delivery. This includes appropriate syringe selection, needleless connectors, flushing solutions, and adherence to sterile technique to prevent complications like bloodstream infections.

**3. The Nursing Process (pp. 111-117)**
The application of the nursing process is central to safe practice. This involves:
*   Assessment: Verifying patient identification, medication orders, and site patency.
*   Diagnosis/Planning: Identifying potential risks, such as extravasation or speed shock.
*   Implementation: Executing the "Rights of Medication Administration."
*   Evaluation: Monitoring the patient for immediate therapeutic effects or adverse reactions.

**4. Clinical Procedures and Documentation (pp. 118-122)**
These pages provide a step-by-step checklist for the IV push procedure. Key safety steps include confirming site patency through a saline flush before and after delivery and observing the specific administration rate required for the medication. Standardized documentation practices are also emphasized to ensure clear communication of the drug, dose, route, time, and patient response.

**5. Learning and Review (pp. 123-130)**
This concluding segment provides educational activities to reinforce learning and a glossary of key terms to ensure technical proficiency in IV medication terminology.

### Summary: Administering Blood Products (Part III)

This section focuses on the professional standards and procedural requirements for the safe administration of blood products. Key content areas include:

**Basic Concepts (p. 133)**
This subsection outlines the physiological rationale for blood transfusions, the various types of blood products, and the specific considerations required to ensure patient safety. It emphasizes the importance of compatibility and recognizing potential transfusion reactions.

**Applying the Nursing Process (p. 153)**
This area details the systematic approach nurses must take before, during, and after a blood transfusion. It highlights:
* Pre-transfusion assessment, including baseline vital signs and verification of physician orders.
* Diligent monitoring for adverse reactions throughout the infusion process.
* The critical nature of patient monitoring and rapid intervention if a reaction occurs.

**Checklist: Administer Blood Products (p. 159)**
A step-by-step procedural guide designed for clinical competency. It covers mandatory safety practices such as the "two-nurse check" for patient and blood unit identification, ensuring proper blood tubing usage, and maintaining strict infusion time limits to prevent bacterial growth.

**Documentation (p. 164)**
Guidance on the legal and clinical necessity of documenting the transfusion process. This includes recording the start/end times, the specific blood product details (unit numbers, expiration dates), the patient's reaction, and the volume infused.

**Learning Activities and Glossary (p. 166-169)**
Integrated resources to reinforce student understanding of blood-related terminology and clinical decision-making.

### Summary of Part IV: Manage Central Lines (pp. 173–230)

Part IV of the text provides a comprehensive guide to the care and management of Central Venous Access Devices (CVADs). The following key areas are addressed:

**1. Foundational Concepts (pp. 175–192)**
This section introduces the purpose and types of central lines. It details the specialized knowledge required to maintain these devices, emphasizing the importance of preventing complications such as infection, occlusion, and catheter migration.

**2. Application of the Nursing Process (pp. 193–209)**
Nurses are instructed on the systematic care of CVADs. This includes:
* Assessing the insertion site for signs of infection or skin breakdown.
* Maintaining patency through proper flushing techniques.
* Implementing evidence-based practices to ensure patient safety while managing infusion lines.

**3. Procedural Checklists (pp. 210–222)**
The text provides step-by-step clinical checklists to standardize high-risk procedures:
* Dressing Changes and Connector Maintenance: Procedures for changing the CVAD dressing and the needleless connector to maintain a sterile field (p. 210).
* Blood Sampling: Protocols for safely obtaining blood samples directly from a CVAD (p. 215).
* Accessing Implanted Devices: Detailed steps for accessing implanted venous access ports (p. 219).

**4. Documentation and Learning (pp. 223–226)**
The final portions of this chapter outline the necessary documentation standards for all CVAD procedures, ensuring accurate clinical records. The section concludes with learning activities and a glossary to reinforce terminology and clinical proficiency.

This section covers Part V, Chapter 5: Insert Nasogastric and Feeding Tubes (p. 231-270). The key points are summarized below:

**Overview and Goals**
Chapter 5 focuses on the clinical procedures required for the placement and removal of nasogastric (NG) and feeding tubes. The content is designed to provide nursing students with both the theoretical foundation and the procedural steps necessary for safe patient care.

**Core Concepts**
The chapter introduces the purpose and indications for NG and feeding tubes, encompassing the basic physiological and clinical rationales for their use in patient populations. It emphasizes the importance of the nursing process—assessment, planning, implementation, and evaluation—when managing patients who require enteral access.

**Procedural Checklists**
The text provides standardized checklists to ensure consistency and patient safety during high-risk tasks:
* Insert a Nasogastric Tube: Guidance on preparation, insertion technique, and verification of placement.
* Remove an NG Tube: Steps to ensure patient comfort and prevent potential complications during withdrawal.

**Documentation and Clinical Application**
The chapter outlines the essential components of clinical documentation for tube procedures, ensuring accountability and continuity of care. Additionally, the section includes learning activities to reinforce comprehension and practical skill development.

**Professional Standards**
All instruction within this chapter aligns with the Wisconsin Technical College System (WTCS) nursing curriculum and the NCLEX-RN Test Plan, ensuring students are prepared for clinical practice and licensure examinations.

The provided text covers Part VI, Chapter 6, which focuses on the management of chest tube drainage systems (pp. 271-320). Below is a summary of the key points:

**Introduction and Basic Concepts**
The chapter introduces the purpose of chest tube drainage systems, which are utilized to restore normal pressure in the pleural space, evacuate air or fluid (such as blood or pus), and allow the lungs to re-expand. Understanding the mechanics of the pleural cavity and the specific function of the drainage device is essential for patient safety.

**Nursing Responsibilities**
Nursing care for patients with chest tubes revolves around constant monitoring and maintenance of the system. Responsibilities include:
* Ensuring the system remains closed and sterile.
* Monitoring drainage output volume and characteristics.
* Assessing the insertion site for signs of infection or subcutaneous emphysema.
* Ensuring the drainage unit remains below the patient's chest level to facilitate gravity drainage and prevent backflow.

**Troubleshooting and Complications**
The text details how to identify and manage common problems, such as air leaks, accidental dislodgement, or tubing disconnection. Nurses must be proficient in troubleshooting clinical deterioration or mechanical failures associated with the drainage apparatus and know the immediate interventions required for complications.

**Checklists and Procedures**
The chapter provides procedural checklists to standardize care, including:
* Managing a closed chest tube drainage system.
* Setting up the drainage system.
* Assisting the healthcare provider with the insertion of a new chest tube.
* Assisting with the clinical removal of a chest tube.

**Documentation and Learning**
Clear documentation is emphasized as a critical safety requirement, ensuring that the patient's respiratory status, the function of the drainage system, and the nature of the output are recorded accurately. Learning activities and a chapter glossary are provided to assist students in mastering this advanced nursing skill.

### Summary: Part VII - Interpret Basic ECG (pp. 321–398)

This section provides a clinical framework for the interpretation and management of electrocardiograms (ECGs) in a nursing context.

**Key Concepts and Methodology**
* **Foundational Knowledge:** The material introduces basic electrophysiology and the systematic steps required to analyze an ECG strip (pp. 322, 338).
* **ECG Analysis:** The text outlines how to identify various dysrhythmias and normal sinus rhythms. Readers are directed to an appendix containing rhythm strips for practice (pp. 347, 388).
* **Clinical Procedures:** Essential protocols are provided for initiating telemetry monitoring, obtaining a 12-lead ECG, providing cardioversion, and administering defibrillation (pp. 368, 373, 376).

**Skill Application**
* **Checklists:** The chapter includes structured observation and performance checklists for nurses to ensure standardized care when interpreting strips or setting up monitoring equipment (pp. 373, 376, 379).
* **Learning Integration:** The section concludes with a "Spotlight Application" and supplemental learning activities to reinforce diagnostic skills and critical thinking regarding cardiac rhythms (pp. 380, 382).

**Assessment**
* **Verification:** An answer key for the learning activities associated with this chapter is provided to allow for self-evaluation of competency in ECG interpretation (p. 414).

The section spanning pages 399-436 of the *Nursing Advanced Skills* textbook provides critical resources to support student learning and course completion. The key components of this section are structured as follows:

**Answer Keys**
Pages 399-414 contain the official answer keys for the learning activities presented throughout the seven chapters of the text. These include:
* Chapter 1: Initiate IV Therapy (p. 399)
* Chapter 2: Administer IV Push Medications (p. 400)
* Chapter 3: Administer Blood Products (p. 404)
* Chapter 4: Manage Central Lines (p. 407)
* Chapter 5: Insert Nasogastric and Feeding Tubes (p. 409)
* Chapter 6: Manage Chest Tube Drainage Systems (p. 410)
* Chapter 7: Interpret Basic ECG (p. 414)

**Master Glossary**
Beginning on page 415, the text provides a comprehensive, consolidated Master Glossary (Appendix A). This resource defines the terminology used across all chapters, ensuring students have a centralized reference for technical nursing vocabulary related to advanced clinical skills such as venipuncture, IV therapy, and ECG interpretation.

**General Overview**
These concluding pages serve as an essential tool for self-assessment, allowing students to verify their understanding of the material. The textbook itself is designed for prelicensure nursing students, with content aligned to the Wisconsin Technical College System (WTCS) curriculum and the NCLEX-RN Test Plan. The entirety of this reference material—along with the rest of the text—is distributed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License, facilitating open access and educational sharing.
Here is a quiz for this lesson.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes the primary purpose of the Open RN 'Nursing Advanced Skills' textbook?
options: To provide a comprehensive list of all medical diagnoses for nursing students.
To offer evidence-based resources and checklists for advanced procedures to prelicensure nursing students.
To serve as a general reference for hospital administration and policy.
To replace the NCLEX-RN exam with an open-source alternative.
questions.explanation: The textbook is designed to provide prelicensure nursing students with evidence-based content and procedural checklists for advanced nursing skills, aligning with the NCLEX-RN test plan.

questions.question: What is the significance of the 'Clinical Judgment' conceptual approach used in the textbook's learning activities?
options: It focuses entirely on memorizing step-by-step procedures to avoid error.
It emphasizes the ability to recognize cues, prioritize hypotheses, and evaluate outcomes, aligning with the NCSBN model.
It requires students to pass a subjective evaluation by their instructor.
It encourages students to focus only on the documentation phase of nursing care.
questions.explanation: The textbook incorporates the NCSBN Clinical Judgment Measurement Model, which aids students in developing critical thinking skills such as recognizing, analyzing, and prioritizing patient data.

questions.question: According to the textbook's licensing information, how can students and faculty legally use the content?
options: They may only print the material for their own personal, non-commercial use.
They are prohibited from changing or adapting any part of the text.
They may share, adapt, and remix the content provided they give appropriate attribution under the CC-BY 4.0 license.
They must pay a royalty fee to the U.S. Department of Education for every copy made.
questions.explanation: The text is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International (CC-BY) license, which explicitly allows sharing, adapting, and remixing as long as proper attribution is provided.

questions.question: When addressing patients in the textbook, why is the singular pronoun 'they' used?
options: It is the required format for all Wisconsin state nursing board documents.
It is an inclusive way to refer to individuals regardless of gender identity, as endorsed by APA style.
It is intended to refer to the patient and their family collectively.
It is a placeholder for students to fill in the patient's specific legal name.
questions.explanation: The text adopts the use of the singular 'they' to be inclusive of all people and to avoid making assumptions about gender, following current APA style guidelines.

questions.question: How are external standards, such as those from the Infusion Nurses Society or QSEN, integrated into the textbook?
options: They are listed in a separate appendix and are optional for student reading.
They serve as the foundation for the content related to procedures, safety, and quality patient care.
They are only included for faculty reference, not for student assessment.
They replace the need for the NCLEX-RN test plan guidelines.
questions.explanation: These standards, including QSEN competencies and Infusion therapy standards, are foundational to the textbook to ensure that students learn procedures that are safe, high-quality, and evidence-based.

cards.front: What is the primary target audience for the 'Nursing Advanced Skills' OER textbook?
Which professional curriculum standards does the 'Nursing Advanced Skills' textbook align with?
cards.back: The Wisconsin Technical College System (WTCS) statewide nursing curriculum and the NCLEX-RN Test Plan.

cards.front: What license is the 'Nursing Advanced Skills' textbook under?
Which nursing organization standards are incorporated into the IV therapy and blood product administration chapters?
What are the six QSEN prelicensure competencies mentioned in the text?
cards.back: Patient-centered care, teamwork and collaboration, evidence-based practice, quality improvement, safety, and informatics.

cards.front: What model does the textbook use in its case studies to help students develop nursing judgment?
cards.back: The NCSBN Clinical Judgment Measurement Model.

cards.front: What are the core technical skills covered in this nursing advanced skills course?
cards.back: IV therapy, blood product administration, central line management, chest tube system management, basic ECG interpretation, and nasogastric/feeding tube insertion.

cards.front: According to the text, why is the singular pronoun 'they' used?
cards.back: To be inclusive of all people and avoid making assumptions about gender when the gender is unknown or irrelevant.

cards.front: What document governs the practice of registered nurses and licensed practical nurses in Wisconsin?
What are the six steps of the NCSBN Clinical Judgment Measurement Model used in the learning activities?
cards.back: Recognize cues, analyze cues, prioritize hypotheses, generate solutions, take action, and evaluate outcomes.

slides.title: Nursing Advanced Skills: Course Overview
bullets: Covers critical clinical procedures for registered nurses.
Aligned with NCLEX-RN standards and evidence-based practice.
Includes IV therapy, central lines, and ECG interpretation.
Part of the Open RN project for nursing education.
slides.notes: This lesson introduces the advanced skills required for professional nursing practice. We will explore core competencies and the evidence-based standards guiding this clinical curriculum.

slides.title: Standards and Conceptual Approach
bullets: Guided by ANA standards and NCLEX-RN test plans.
Incorporates Infusion Nurses Society and QSEN competencies.
Focuses on clinical judgment and evidence-based practice.
Emphasizes cultural competency and ethical nursing care.
slides.notes: Our curriculum is grounded in professional standards to ensure safe, quality care. We emphasize developing clinical judgment through evidence-based practice and cultural humility.

slides.title: Chapter 1: Initiate IV Therapy
bullets: Covers peripheral IV access and venipuncture techniques.
Includes standardized checklists for IV insertion and removal.
Focuses on documentation and specialized infusion safety.
Presents case studies to refine clinical decision-making.
slides.notes: Chapter 1 covers the foundational skills of IV therapy. Students will master venipuncture techniques and the clinical documentation required for safe infusion administration.

slides.title: Chapter 2: Administer IV Push Medications
bullets: Fundamentals of IV push medication administration.
Reviewing essential equipment and nursing process application.
Ensuring safety through standardized medication procedures.
Documentation requirements for IV push delivery.
slides.notes: Administering medication via IV push requires precision and strict safety protocols. We will review the necessary equipment and integrate the nursing process into each step.

slides.title: Chapter 3: Administer Blood Products
bullets: Basic concepts of blood component therapy.
Applying the nursing process to transfusion management.
Checklists for the safe administration of blood products.
Clinical monitoring and documentation guidelines.
slides.notes: Blood product administration is a high-stakes nursing skill. This chapter focuses on patient safety, strict monitoring, and the systematic process for transfusions.

slides.title: Chapter 4: Manage Central Lines
bullets: Concepts of Central Venous Access Devices (CVADs).
Maintenance: Dressing changes and connector care.
Sampling blood and accessing implanted venous devices.
Comprehensive documentation for central line management.
slides.notes: Managing central lines involves complex maintenance and strict infection control. We will cover the specific procedures for dressing changes and blood sampling from these devices.

slides.title: Chapter 5: Nasogastric and Feeding Tubes
bullets: Concepts and clinical indications for tube placement.
Step-by-step guidance for NG tube insertion.
Safe procedures for removing nasogastric tubes.
Standard documentation protocols for enteral access.
slides.notes: Inserting and managing nasogastric tubes requires comfort with anatomical landmarks and safety checks. We will walk through the insertion and removal processes carefully.

slides.title: Chapter 6: Manage Chest Tube Systems
bullets: Nursing responsibilities for chest tube drainage.
Troubleshooting common system problems and complications.
Checklists for setup, maintenance, and system removal.
Assisting with clinical chest tube procedures.
slides.notes: Chest tube drainage systems require close monitoring to ensure patient stability. We will focus on troubleshooting and the nurse's role in system management and removal.

slides.title: Chapter 7: Interpret Basic ECG
bullets: Systematic approach to interpreting electrocardiograms.
Reviewing common ECG patterns and dysrhythmias.
Initiating telemetry and 12-lead ECG procedures.
Overview of cardioversion and defibrillation concepts.
slides.notes: Interpreting basic ECGs is essential for cardiac monitoring. We will learn a systematic method for analysis and identify key dysrhythmias and interventions.
